rfi_text: This is a vetting request for further information under section 45 of the Building Act 2004. Owner contact details have not been provided in full. Selwyn District Council requires complete owner contact information before an application can be accepted. Please provide owner full name, address, phone, and email. Note: applications cannot be accepted until all owner details are provided. The 20 working day clock will reset to zero on receipt of this RFI response.
expected_response: Updated Form 2 with full owner contact details. Reply via AlphaOne email only (e.g. 21XXXX@sdc.abcs.co.nz).
source: SDC Requirements For All Applications + Processing of Your Consent

rfi_text: The proposed dwelling/principal building/alteration has a floor area greater than 25m² but the application does not include an SDC Flooding Assessment Certificate (FAC). An FAC is required for new principal buildings, new residential units, or additions of 25m² or more. Please obtain and submit the FAC before the application can be accepted.
expected_response: SDC Flooding Assessment Certificate (FAC) attached to the application via AlphaOne.
source: SDC Requirements For All Applications §pre_application_approvals + SDC How to Supply Your Supporting Documents

rfi_text: The application is for a multi-unit residential development in an urban zone but does not include Engineering Acceptance from the Development Engineering team. Engineering Acceptance is required prior to lodging this consent. Please contact development.engineer@selwyn.govt.nz to obtain Engineering Acceptance and submit the stamped plans + acceptance letter with this application. (Note: from 7 April 2025, minor residential units in residential zones are exempt — except in MRZ.)
expected_response: Engineering Acceptance letter + stamped plans from SDC Development Engineering, attached to the application.
source: SDC Requirements For All Applications §pre_application_approvals + SDC Engineering Acceptance

rfi_text: The PLG1 form (National Environmental Standard for contaminants in soil + earthworks within the Plains Flood Management Overlay) has not been provided. This form is required where the land is on, near, or potentially HAIL-listed, or where earthworks may displace floodwater. Please complete the PLG1 form using the PLG-1A guidance and submit.
expected_response: Completed PLG1 form (PDF, 322 KB) addressing HAIL/earthworks status.

rfi_text: This application does not include a geotechnical or shallow soil investigation report. Most building consent applications in Selwyn require one. The site is in an area susceptible to liquefaction or other natural hazards (per the ECan 2012 liquefaction hazard report). Please provide either a shallow soil investigation report (with scala penetrometer test results) or a full geotechnical report as appropriate.
expected_response: Geotech report (or shallow soil investigation) with site investigation findings and foundation recommendations.

rfi_text: The application proposes installation of a new septic tank but does not include the site-specific design or the corresponding Environment Canterbury (ECan) approval. SDC cannot issue a building consent without confirmation of a lawful outfall for foul water. Please submit the site-specific septic design and ECan approval in full.
expected_response: Site-specific septic system design + ECan approval letter for the disposal-to-ground activity.

rfi_text: The application is for an addition that includes additional bedrooms, but does not address the capacity of the existing septic tank. Please provide professional advice (with calculations) confirming whether the existing system can cope with the additional capacity, or include design + ECan approval for system replacement. SDC cannot issue consent without confirmation that the lawful outfall for foul water continues to comply.
expected_response: Capacity assessment from a drainage engineer or designer, plus ECan approval for any system change.

rfi_text: Documents submitted are not searchable PDFs. Per SDC's How to Supply Your Supporting Documents guidance, all documents must be searchable PDFs. Please convert and resubmit. Also confirm: documents are combined into the files in the specified order, and one single file is provided in each upload area.
expected_response: Resubmit all documents as searchable PDFs, combined per the specified upload structure.
source: SDC How to Supply Your Supporting Documents

rfi_text: This RFI response does not include a document transmittal or updated cover page detailing documentation names, sheet numbers, and current version references. Per SDC RFI response requirements, your response is deemed incomplete and the 20-day clock will not restart. Please reply with: (a) revision clouds + version numbering on changes, (b) transmittal/cover page, (c) attachments as PDFs printable at A3 maximum.
expected_response: Re-submit RFI response with required document transmittal, revision clouds, version numbering, and A3-compliant scale.

rfi_text: The proposed building work includes specified systems (e.g. fire alarm, lift, sprinkler, mechanical ventilation). The Specified Systems Information form (DOCX, 105 KB) has not been completed. Please complete this form using the Specified Systems Guidance Document (DOCX, 1761 KB) and submit, including proposed inspection, maintenance, and reporting procedures for each system.
expected_response: Completed Specified Systems Information form covering each system with inspection/maintenance/reporting procedures and performance standards.

rfi_text: The specification references an alternative cladding system (not commonly used / new to market). Please provide manufacturer's literature and background information to support compliance, including specific BRANZ appraisal numbers, manufacturer technical manual versions, and any relevant CodeMark certificates. Supply only the relevant pages (not the full product brochure).
expected_response: Manufacturer documentation pages + BRANZ appraisal + CodeMark certificate (if any) referenced from the specification.

rfi_text: This application includes specific engineering design but does not include verifiable evidence of Building Code compliance for that design. Please provide a Producer Statement for Design (PS1) with supporting calculations, sketches/drawings, or a combination of these. Note: PS1 alone is not sufficient — backing calculations are expected.
expected_response: PS1 from registered engineer + supporting calculations + drawings.
source: SDC Requirements For All Applications §what_to_supply + Other Application Considerations §professional_opinions

rfi_text: The application relies on a professional opinion / design feature report / subject matter expert opinion to demonstrate compliance. Per Section 51 of the Building Act 2004 and SDC's published guidance, professional opinions have no legal status under the Building Act and will be assessed on merit. Please provide verifiable evidence (e.g. calculations) supporting the opinion, so we can be 'satisfied on reasonable grounds' that the work will comply.
expected_response: Backing calculations or verifiable evidence supporting the professional opinion.

rfi_text: This building will require a Compliance Schedule under Section 100 of the Building Act 2004 due to the inclusion of specified systems. Please provide draft Compliance Schedule content including inspection, maintenance, and reporting procedures, and the performance standards for each system.
expected_response: Draft Compliance Schedule with full IM&R procedures + performance standards for each system.
source: SDC Specific Approvals §specified_systems + WDC LGIR Step 5 (Compliance Schedule)

rfi_text: The proposed pool barrier does not comply with Acceptable Solution F9/AS1. The junctions between the boundary fence and internal fencing create horizontal projections greater than 10mm within the 900mm non-climbable zone (paragraph 2.1.7), and access from the adjacent property is not adequately addressed. Please either (a) revise to comply with F9/AS1, or (b) submit an alternative solution demonstrating compliance with the performance requirements F9.3.1 and F9.3.3 (no permanent objects/projections on the outside that could assist children in negotiating the barrier).
expected_response: Either revised plans complying with F9/AS1 or an alternative solution with supporting analysis of all permanent projections and climb-assist features.

rfi_text: The plans and specifications do not demonstrate compliance with E2 - External Moisture for the proposed cladding system. Please identify the compliance pathway (E2/AS1 acceptable solution, alternative solution, or verification method) and provide supporting documentation including critical junction details.
expected_response: E2 compliance pathway statement + cladding manufacturer details + junction drawings.
source: SDC Requirements For All Applications + general E2 compliance

rfi_text: This application is required to be sent to the Fire and Emergency NZ (FENZ) Fire Engineering Unit for design review under Section 46 of the Building Act 2004 (FENZ types per the NZ Gazette). The 20-day statutory clock continues, but FENZ has 10 working days to provide feedback. We will share their advice once received. No action required from you at this stage unless FENZ raises issues.
expected_response: No applicant action required initially; respond if FENZ feedback necessitates design changes.

rfi_text: This application affects a historic place / wāhi tapu area on the New Zealand Heritage List (Rārangi Kōrero). SDC has notified Heritage NZ within 5 days as required. The decision to grant/refuse remains with SDC and is based on Building Code compliance. Please confirm any cultural/historical considerations are addressed in the design and supply additional documentation if Heritage NZ raises concerns.
expected_response: Confirmation of any consultation with Heritage NZ; if applicable, response to Heritage NZ feedback.

rfi_text: The product '[X]' specified in the documentation is identified as relying on a BRANZ appraisal but the appraisal number is not stated. Please add references in the specification to BRANZ appraisal numbers and manufacturer technical manual versions for clarity.
expected_response: Updated specification with BRANZ appraisal numbers and manual versions cited.

rfi_text: The product / method '[X]' specified in this application has been the subject of a MBIE warning or ban (effective from [date]). Please refer to MBIE's published warnings/bans and either substitute a compliant alternative or provide evidence that the warning/ban does not apply to your specific use case.
expected_response: Either revised specification with compliant product, or MBIE ban-applicability statement.

rfi_text: This is a Development Engineering RFI (separate from the building consent RFI process). The proposed multi-unit development does not demonstrate a lawful outfall for foul water / stormwater. Please provide: (a) detailed plans of proposed connection, (b) design report with construction methodology, (c) ECOP-compliance evidence, (d) CPEng or SQEP certification. Submit to development.engineer@selwyn.govt.nz.
expected_response: DE acceptance application with all four required attachments.

rfi_text: This pool installation does not include a backflow preventer. SDC requires a backflow preventer to ensure pool water cannot flow back into the water supply and contaminate it. Please update the plumbing design and specification.
expected_response: Revised plumbing schematic showing backflow preventer at the pool connection.
source: WDC Building Inspections fact sheet §pool (applicable principle, equivalent SDC rule)

rfi_text: The supporting documents are not provided in the order required by SDC's How to Supply Your Supporting Documents guidance. Please re-submit with documents in the specified order: Record of Title → FAC → Engineering Acceptance → PLG1 → RBW Memorandum → Plans → Specifications → Supporting Documents (geotech, PS1s, BRANZ/CodeMark, product literature). Each upload area should contain a single combined PDF.
expected_response: Resubmission with documents combined per specified order.
source: SDC How to Supply Your Supporting Documents

rfi_text: The Specified Systems Information form has been provided, but performance standards for each system are not included. SDC notes industry best practice is to include performance standards for each specified system. Please add performance standards.
expected_response: Updated Specified Systems form with performance standards listed for each system.

rfi_text: The site is in the Plains Flood Management Overlay. Finished Floor Level documentation does not address potential flooding hazards or freeboard requirements. Please provide a flood-level assessment and design FFL accordingly. (For rural properties: ECan flood assessment report required.)
expected_response: Flood-level assessment + FFL design with freeboard, supported by ECan flood assessment report (rural).
source: SDC Requirements For All Applications + WDC Building on a Rural Site §floor_level

rfi_text: The septic system design references discharge to ground but the corresponding ECan approval has not been provided. Please contact ECan to discuss the regional rules that apply to your project and obtain their approval for the disposal activity. SDC cannot issue building consent without confirmation of a lawful outfall.
expected_response: ECan written approval letter for the discharge-to-ground activity.

rfi_text: The application for a solar hot water system does not include a plumbing schematic, hot water cylinder specifications, or safe tray details. Please provide all three.
expected_response: Plumbing schematic + cylinder spec sheet + safe tray detail drawing.

rfi_text: On final review prior to grant, additional clarification is required. While the application has substantively complied, the following inconsistency between drawings and specification needs resolution: [item]. Please provide updated documentation showing alignment.
expected_response: Reconciled drawings and specification.
source: Synthesised — SDC consent documents review per Processing of Your Consent §consent_documents_created
